text:
<image>
User: Describe the energy transformation in this system.
Assistant: As the ball bounces, Potential Energy (at the peak) converts to Kinetic Energy (at the bottom). The decaying height indicates energy loss due to air resistance and inelastic collision with the floor (Non-Conservative Forces).

<image>
User: What determines if the block slides down?
Assistant: The block slides if the component of Gravity parallel to the slope (Fg_parallel) exceeds the Static Friction force (Ff). Dynamically, F_net = Fg_sin(theta) - mu * F_N.

<image>
User: Explain why this system is in equilibrium.
Assistant: The system is balanced because the Net Torque is zero. The Torque (Tau = Force x Distance) applied by the large mass on the left equals the combined Torque of the two smaller masses on the right.
